elements.title: a world-championship-medalist swimmer
elements.contents: ... that a world-championship-medalist swimmer (pictured) called his sport "one of the dullest in existence"?

elements.title: Henderson Place Historic District
elements.contents: ... that the houses in New York City's Henderson Place Historic District have been described as "gingerbread-type" and likened to dollhouse architecture?

elements.title: Erwan Kepoa Falé
elements.contents: ... that actor Erwan Kepoa Falé said that sex was his escape from grief after the death of his father?

elements.title: 2018 regency election
elements.contents: ... that "almost every road in Sikka had campaign posts" during the 2018 regency election campaign?

elements.title: The World Is to Dig
elements.contents: ... that the title of the 2026 album The World Is to Dig was inspired by a 1952 children's book?

elements.title: John Kinsey
elements.contents: ... that John Kinsey remained speaker of the New Jersey colonial assembly despite moving to Pennsylvania?

elements.title: Tele7ABC
elements.contents: ... that nighttime programming on Romania's Tele7ABC in 2000 consisted of the poet Adrian Păunescu and a "crumpled blue curtain"?

elements.title: when James Baker was U.S. secretary of state
elements.contents: ... that, when James Baker was U.S. secretary of state, he banned Benjamin Netanyahu from the department's headquarters?

elements.title: Laura Taylor
elements.contents: ... that softball player Laura Taylor scored only four home runs in high school, but then became one of the top home-run-hitters in college history?
